site.description: A creative studio for tools, experiments, and visual transmissions.
site.metaDescription: A creative studio for tools, experiments, and visual transmissions. Built with craft.

about.title: About
about.meta_description: Mentria is a small creative studio. Privacy-first tools, games, and AI that run entirely in your browser — no servers, no accounts, no tracking.
about.s1_p1: Mentria is a small creative studio building software the way zines used to get printed — locally, deliberately, and without permission. The site you're reading is a progressive web app. Everything inside it — the tools, the games, the AI — runs on the device in front of you. There is no backend pinging your prompts to a server farm. There are no trackers measuring how long you stared at the homepage.
about.s1_p2: We make tools that respect you: an in-browser tokenizer, a WebGPU inference engine, a handful of small games, a transmissions feed of writing. No accounts, no email capture, no cookie banner. If something here is useful to you, it stays useful when your wifi cuts out — and stays useful five years from now, when whichever startup you signed up to has pivoted, lapsed, or vanished.
about.stack_2: vanilla js, no framework, single npm dep
about.stack_5: pwa, offline-capable, hosted on github pages
about.data_section_lede: Everything you create here lives on your device. Back it up so a browser wipe doesn't take it with it.
about.data_persist_status_pending: Storage may be evicted under pressure
about.data_export_lede: Choose a passphrase, download an encrypted file. Lose the passphrase and the backup is unrecoverable.
about.data_import_lede: Pick a backup file and enter the passphrase you used.
about.sync_section_lede: Pair two devices with a one-time code. Data syncs peer-to-peer, end-to-end encrypted. Our relay never sees your data.
about.sync_pair_lede: Show this code on the other device. The code is the encryption key — don't share it elsewhere.
about.sync_join_lede: Enter the code from your other device to sync.
about.sync_code_invalid: Code too short — paste the full code
about.sync_scan_button: 📷 Scan QR from the other device
about.sync_scan_unsupported: QR scanning isn't supported here — type the code instead.
about.sync_scan_denied: Camera access denied. Allow camera permission and try again.
about.sync_scan_no_camera: No camera available on this device.
about.sync_scan_secure_required: Camera access needs HTTPS. Use the live site, not a local URL.
about.identity_lede_none: An encrypted secret on this device that lets your other devices auto-link. Set it up once, restore from your backup elsewhere, and the rest is automatic.
about.identity_lede_locked: Identity is set up on this device. Enter your passphrase to unlock it.
about.identity_lede_unlocked: Identity unlocked. Tap "Sync this device" — others with the same identity (restored from your backup) will join automatically.
about.identity_too_short: Passphrase must be at least 8 characters.
about.identity_unlock_failed: Unlock failed — wrong passphrase or corrupt vault.
about.identity_setup_reminder: Identity created. Now go to "Your data" above and download an encrypted backup — restoring it on another device gives that device the same identity.

common.heavy_download: This downloads about 490 MB of model data and you appear to be on a slow or data-saving connection. Continue?
common.download_postponed: Download paused — reload the page to start.
common.storage_full: Couldn't save — your browser storage is full. Free up space to keep your changes.

tools_page.title: Utility Tools
tools_page.lede: Simple, focused utilities that work offline. Each one does one thing well.

feed_page.title: Feed
feed_page.lede: Visual transmissions from the Mentria studio.
feed_page.empty_msg: > no transmissions yet. check back soon.

pwa.banner_desc: Add to home screen — works offline.
pwa.ios_step1: Tap the Share button at the bottom of the screen
pwa.macos_step2: From the menu bar, choose File → Add to Dock…
pwa.chrome_step1: Click the install icon in your browser's address bar.
pwa.chrome_step2: Or open the menu (⋮) and choose Install Mentria.
pwa.note: Once installed, Mentria runs offline and your local data stays on your device.

ai-chat.title: AI Chat
ai-chat.lede: Qwen3.5 running locally in your browser. No server, no API keys. Supports vision.
ai-chat.tldr: AI Chat runs Qwen3.5 locally in your browser via WebGPU. Your messages and any uploaded images never leave your device — no server, no API key, no account. Supports vision input. The model loads on first click; subsequent visits start instantly from local cache.

base64-codec.title: Base64 Codec
base64-codec.lede: Encode and decode text or files to and from Base64. Supports images, documents, and any file type. Everything runs locally in your browser.
base64-codec.tldr: A privacy-first encoder and decoder for text and files. Encoding and decoding both happen in your browser using vanilla JavaScript — no upload, no server. Drag a file in or paste a Base64 string and copy or download the result. Works offline, with no size limit beyond browser memory.

extensions.title: Extensions
extensions.lede: Install private single-file extensions that live only on this device. They appear in your launcher and terminal — and never on the public site.
extensions.tldr: A bring-your-own-tool platform. An extension is one HTML file with an embedded manifest; install it here and it gets a tile in your launcher, optional terminal commands, and its own local storage that rides your encrypted backup. Extensions run with full access on this device, so only install files from people you trust. Nothing about them is ever uploaded.

coin-flip.title: Coin Flip
coin-flip.lede: Heads or tails — flip a coin instantly. Run a best-of series for tiebreakers. Works offline.
coin-flip.tldr: A no-frills coin flip backed by the browser's secure RNG (Web Crypto). Heads or tails is decided locally; nothing is logged or sent to a server. Keeps a running history per session. Useful for quick decisions, fair starts, or settling tabletop arguments — instant on every device.

color-picker.title: Color Picker
color-picker.lede: Pick any color, view its HEX / RGB / HSL values, and generate harmonious palettes. Works offline.
color-picker.tldr: Pick, sample, and save colors entirely in your browser. Outputs HEX, RGB, and HSL side by side. Saved swatches persist via localStorage; nothing is uploaded. Click into the picker, copy any value to clipboard, or build a small palette for design or code. Works offline.

countdown-timer.title: Countdown Timer
countdown-timer.lede: Set a duration or pick a target time. Up to 4 independent timers with alarm sound.
countdown-timer.tldr: Run up to four countdown timers in parallel with optional system notifications when they finish, even when the tab is hidden. Set a duration or a target date and time, name each timer, save presets. The audio alarm and notification fire from your browser; nothing is logged or sent to a server.

decision-wheel.title: Decision Wheel
decision-wheel.lede: Can't decide? Spin the wheel and let fate choose. Works offline.
decision-wheel.tldr: Spin a customizable wheel to pick from your own list of options. Add or remove choices on the fly; the outcome is selected locally with the browser's RNG. No accounts, no trackers, no server. Useful for restaurant picks, task assignment, or anything you would otherwise flip a coin for.

flappy.title: Flappy
flappy.lede: A paper plane threading a stack of command prompts. Tap, click, or press SPACE to flap. Don't crash the prompt.
flappy.tldr: A browser-native take on Flappy Bird with a synthwave-terminal aesthetic. Pure HTML5 Canvas — no install, no signup, no third-party scripts. Game state, high score, and audio all run client-side and stay on your device. Tap, click, or press space to flap. Works offline once cached.

invoice.title: Invoice Generator
invoice.lede: Build clean, single-page invoices in your browser. Auto-saves as you type. Print to A4 or export to JSON.
invoice.tldr: Build clean A4 invoices in your browser and save them as PDF via the browser's print dialog. Add line items, currency, bank details, footnotes; auto-saves to localStorage so refreshes do not lose data. Import or export the whole invoice as JSON. No accounts, no server — every byte stays on your device.

markdown-pdf.title: Markdown → PDF
markdown-pdf.lede: Write markdown, see a live preview, and download a clean professional PDF. Auto-saves to your browser. No data leaves your device.
markdown-pdf.tldr: A markdown editor with live preview and one-click PDF export through your browser's print dialog. Imports and exports .md files, auto-saves drafts locally, supports GitHub-flavored markdown including tables and task lists. No upload, no account, no server — everything runs in pure client-side JavaScript and works offline.

minesweeper.title: Minesweeper
minesweeper.lede: Classic logic puzzle in terminal dress. Click to dig, right-click (or F-mode) to flag. Numbers count adjacent mines. Don't pop the pink ones.
minesweeper.tldr: Classic Minesweeper rebuilt with a synthwave terminal aesthetic. Difficulty presets (easy, medium, hard, extreme), keyboard and touch controls, persistent best times via localStorage. No installs, no signup, no analytics; the game logic and timer all run locally in your browser. Works offline once the page loads.

quick-notes.title: Quick Notes
quick-notes.lede: A minimal scratchpad that lives in your browser. Create multiple notes, auto-saved on every keystroke.
quick-notes.tldr: A keyboard-first notepad that auto-saves every keystroke to your browser's localStorage. Multiple notes, instant search, dark synthwave aesthetic. Notes never leave your device — no sync, no accounts, no server. Use it as a scratchpad for tasks, snippets, or thoughts you will come back to in a tab.

model.degrade: {from} isn’t supported on this device — using {to}
model.failed: Couldn’t run an on-device model on this device.
model.chooseHint: Your device can run up to {name}. Bigger models are smarter but download more.

quote.title: Motivational Quote
quote.lede: Original quotes generated by AI, running locally in your browser. No data leaves your device.
quote.tldr: Motivational Quote generates original aphorisms with a Qwen3.5 model fine-tuned on curated quote data, running entirely in your browser via WebGPU. No server, no API key, no account. The model loads on first visit and is cached locally for instant reuse.

totp.title: TOTP
totp.lede: Two-factor authentication codes that live in your browser, encrypted at rest. Nothing on a server.
totp.tldr: Standards-compliant TOTP (RFC 6238) running entirely in your browser. Secrets are encrypted at rest with AES-GCM-256; a master key is wrapped by both a passphrase (PBKDF2-SHA256 × 600k) and optionally a WebAuthn PRF-derived key for Touch ID / Windows Hello unlock. Mandatory encrypted JSON backup before the first secret saves — losing your only authenticator means lockout otherwise. Works offline. No accounts, no sync server.

radio.title: Infinite Radio
radio.lede: AI-generated epic cinematic music, streaming infinitely. Learns your taste locally — no data leaves your device.
radio.tldr: Infinite AI-generated cinematic music streaming in your browser. Tracks come from a public catalog and play through the browser's native HTML5 audio element (crossfaded between two players); your listening history and likes stay on-device in IndexedDB to learn your taste locally over time. No accounts, no tracking, no server-side personalization — pure client-side.

tetris.title: Tetris
tetris.lede: Stack tetrominoes. Clear lines. Speed ramps every 10 lines. Pieces in cyan, purple, pink, amber, green — the Mentria palette.
tetris.tldr: Browser-native Tetris with a terminal aesthetic. HTML5 Canvas, accurate piece spawning with Super Rotation System logic, hold queue, ghost piece, level-based gravity. High score saved to localStorage. No install, no signup, no third-party scripts — game logic and timing all run client-side and the game works offline.

search.title: Search
search.lede: Find tools and feed posts. Type any query — keyword or natural language.
search.tldr: Site-wide search backed by a build-time index of every tool and feed post in 5 languages. Default mode is instant fuzzy keyword matching across titles, descriptions, and TL;DRs — no model download required, no server round-trip. When the Qwen3.5 model is already cached locally (after using AI Chat or Quote), an optional AI summary appears above the results.

step-counter.title: Step Counter
step-counter.lede: Count your steps on a walk, right in the browser. Uses your phone's motion sensor — nothing is uploaded.
step-counter.tldr: A session-based pedometer that reads your phone's accelerometer through the DeviceMotion API and counts steps with on-device peak detection. Start a session and walk with the screen on — it tracks steps, distance, pace, and a daily goal ring. All processing stays in the browser: no account, no upload, no background tracking. Desktops and devices without a motion sensor show a disabled state.

qr-scanner.title: QR & Barcode Scanner + Generator
qr-scanner.lede: Scan QR codes and barcodes, or generate a QR code from any link or text — all on-device, nothing uploaded.
qr-scanner.tldr: Two tools in one, both fully on-device. Scan mode reads QR codes and barcodes from your camera or an uploaded image: it uses the browser's native BarcodeDetector when available (Chromium, with the widest format support) and falls back to a built-in JavaScript decoder elsewhere, so QR scanning works on Safari and Firefox too. Generate mode turns any link or text into a QR code with a built-in encoder. Nothing is uploaded and there is no server round-trip — decoded results can be copied or opened, and generated codes downloaded as PNG, copied, or shared.

breakout.title: Breakout
breakout.lede: Brick-buster meets paddle game. Catch falling powerups, clear the wall, survive levels that get faster and meaner. Runs locally — no data leaves your device.
breakout.tldr: A Breakout-style brick-buster: bounce the ball off your paddle to clear walls of bricks across levels that speed up, catching falling powerups (and dodging the bad ones). Built on HTML5 Canvas with deterministic physics and mouse, keyboard, and touch controls. Score, lives, and the high-score table all live in client-side JavaScript — no third-party scripts, no server, and it works offline once cached.

sudoku.title: Sudoku
sudoku.lede: Logic puzzle in terminal dress. Unique-solution generator, four difficulties, pencil marks. Runs locally — no data leaves your device.
sudoku.tldr: Classic 9×9 Sudoku rebuilt with a synthwave terminal aesthetic. A backtracking generator guarantees exactly one solution at four difficulties (easy → expert); pencil-mark notes, peer + same-number highlighting, live conflict detection, three mistakes before game over, and up to three hints. Best completion times per difficulty are saved on-device. Keyboard, mouse, and touch all work. No installs, no signup, no analytics — the puzzle generation and solving all run locally in your browser, and it works offline once the page loads.

ludo.title: Ludo
ludo.lede: Four tokens home wins. Roll a six to break out, capture on contact, dodge to the safe stars. Play the bots or pass & play.
ludo.tldr: The classic cross-and-circle race game, rebuilt on HTML5 canvas with a synthwave terminal aesthetic. Roll a six to release a token, move clockwise, land on an opponent to send it home, and rest on the safe stars. A 6, a capture, or reaching the center grants another roll. Play 2–4 players against built-in bots or pass-and-play on one device. No installs, no signup, no analytics — all dice and AI run locally in your browser, and it works offline.

chess.title: Chess
chess.lede: Local engine, hot-seat, and online play — no servers. Full rules with castling, en passant, promotion; an alpha-beta engine with four strengths, eval bar, and hints.
chess.tldr: A complete chess implementation that runs entirely in your browser. The rules engine covers castling, en passant, promotion, check/checkmate/stalemate, and draws (50-move, insufficient material, threefold repetition), with full SAN move notation. Play hot-seat on one device, against a built-in alpha-beta engine (four strengths from Beginner to Brutal, running in a Web Worker so the UI never freezes) with a live evaluation bar, candidate lines, and move hints — or online against a friend over an encrypted peer-to-peer connection using a share-code (our relay only brokers the connection; moves flow device-to-device). Drag or tap to move, scrub the move history, flip the board, undo, and resign. No accounts, no analytics, works offline.

annotate-image.title: Annotate Image
annotate-image.lede: Upload an image and get an instant AI description, alt-text, transcription, or tags — with tested one-tap prompts. Runs locally in your browser, nothing uploaded.
annotate-image.tldr: A focused, vision-powered companion to AI Chat: drop in an image and a tested prompt runs automatically — no need to write one. Switch between Describe, Read text (OCR), Alt-text, and Objects/tags with one tap. It runs the same on-device Qwen3.5 vision model as AI Chat (shared cache — if you've used AI Chat, no extra download), entirely in your browser via WebGPU. Nothing is uploaded; the image never leaves your device. Drag-and-drop, paste, file-pick, or 'open with' from your file manager once installed. Copy or download the result. WebGPU required (recent Chrome/Edge, or Safari 26+).

tetris.modal_intro: Move with ← →, rotate with ↑, soft drop with ↓, hard drop with SPACE. C to hold a piece.

Steer the paper plane through gaps in the command prompts.

quick-notes.lock_title: Encrypt this note with your device passkey
quick-notes.locked_body: Its contents are encrypted. Unlock with your device passkey to view and edit.
quick-notes.confirm_lock: Locking encrypts this note with your device's passkey. If you lose that passkey or clear this browser's data, the note can't be recovered. Lock it?
quick-notes.confirm_remove_lock: Remove encryption from this note? It will be stored as plain text again.
quick-notes.lock_failed: Couldn't lock the note — the device passkey was unavailable.
quick-notes.unlock_failed: Couldn't unlock the note — the passkey didn't match or was unavailable.
quick-notes.prf_unsupported: This browser or device can't use a passkey for encryption. Try a recent version of Chrome, Edge, or Safari.

step-counter.denied_body: Re-enable motion access in your browser settings, then reload the page.
step-counter.unsupported_body: Step counting needs a phone or tablet with an accelerometer. This device doesn't expose one.
step-counter.screen_hint: Keep the screen on and this tab open while you walk.
step-counter.confirm_reset: Reset today's step count to zero?
step-counter.insecure_body: Motion sensors only work over HTTPS. Open this tool on the live site (https://mentria.ai). A local dev server reached by LAN IP over plain HTTP cannot expose the sensor.

qr-scanner.note_insecure: The camera needs a secure (HTTPS) connection. You can still scan an image file below.
qr-scanner.note_denied: Camera access was blocked. Allow it in your browser settings, or scan an image file instead.

qr-scanner.note_camera_error: The camera could not be started. You can still scan an image file.

totp.locked_lede_unlock: Enter your passphrase to unlock your TOTP vault.

totp.unlock_failed: Unlock failed — wrong passphrase or corrupt vault.

totp.scan_invalid_qr: That QR code is not an otpauth://totp/ URI. Try again with a TOTP setup QR.

ludo.rules_body:
• roll a 6 to move a token out of base
• move tokens clockwise by the dice value
• land on an opponent to send it home
• ★ stars & start cells are safe
• a 6, a capture, or reaching center grants another roll
• get all 4 tokens to the center to win

chess.share_code: share this code with your opponent
chess.host_note: you play white — they join with the code
chess.no_moves_yet: no moves yet — make your move

annotate-image.err_no_webgpu: WebGPU not available — try a recent Chrome/Edge, or Safari 26+
annotate-image.bad_file: that doesn't look like an image
annotate-image.drop_title: drop, paste, or choose an image
annotate-image.drop_sub: png · jpg · webp · gif — stays on your device
annotate-image.sys_prompt: You are Mentria's local image annotator, a vision model running in the browser. Look carefully at the image and answer the request directly, accurately, and concisely. Describe only what is actually visible — never invent details that aren't there.
annotate-image.prompt_describe: Describe this image in detail — the main subject, the setting, colors, and any notable details. Write 2 to 4 clear sentences. Only describe what is actually visible.
annotate-image.prompt_ocr: Read every piece of text in this image and transcribe it exactly as written, preserving the original line order and line breaks. Include all headings, labels, numbers, and fine print. Do not add any commentary, notes, summaries, or explanation — output the transcribed text and nothing else. Only if the image genuinely contains no text at all, reply: (no text found).
annotate-image.prompt_alt: In one brief sentence of at most 20 words, describe this image for a screen-reader user. Capture only the single most important thing shown — do not list every detail or transcribe text. Output just that one short sentence and nothing else.
annotate-image.prompt_tags: Look closely at the image and list its contents as short, comma-separated keyword tags: the main subjects, objects, people, colors, and setting. If the image is mostly text or a document, tag it that way (for example: text, document, screenshot). Give 5 to 12 tags. Always produce tags — never refuse or explain. Output only the comma-separated tags.
